founder: 李·亚历山大·麦昆（Lee Alexander McQueen）
current_slogan: Alexander McQueen — 前卫设计，极致工艺
description_zh: 亚历山大·麦昆（Alexander McQueen）是英国最具影响力的奢侈时装品牌之一，由传奇设计师李·亚历山大·麦昆（Lee Alexander McQueen）于1992年在伦敦创立。麦昆以其戏剧化的时装秀、精湛的剪裁技艺和黑暗浪漫的设计美学闻名于世，被誉为英国时尚的坏小子和设计界的鬼才。麦昆毕业于伦敦中央圣马丁艺术与设计学院，其毕业作品被著名造型师伊莎贝拉·布罗全数购买，一举成名。1996年至2001年间，麦昆在经营自己品牌的同时担任纪梵希创意总监，成为时尚界最具影响力的人物之一。他的标志性设计包括包屁裤、骷髅头丝巾和超低腰裤，这些设计重新定义了时尚的边界。麦昆的时装秀常常以剧场级的叙事方式呈现，融合了历史、科技、自然和哥特元素，如1999年春夏系列的机器人喷绘裙、2006年秋冬的凯特·莫斯全息投影等经典时刻。2010年亚历山大·麦昆不幸离世后，其得力助手莎拉·伯顿接任创意总监，延续并发展了品牌独特的设计语言，推出了多个备受赞誉的系列。品牌以其卓越的定制工艺、创新的面料处理和充满力量感的设计著称，是红毯和颁奖典礼上的常客。麦昆的作品被纽约大都会艺术博物馆和伦敦V&A博物馆永久收藏，被视为当代时尚艺术的重要组成部分。产品线涵盖女装、男装、配饰、香水等多个领域。品牌在高级定制时装领域享有盛誉，其精湛的刺绣工艺和立体剪裁技术在业界独树一帜。McQueen的每一场时装秀都是一个完整的艺术叙事，从舞台设计到音乐选择都经过精心策划。2023年，莎拉·伯顿离任后，肖恩·麦吉瑞（Seán McGirr）接任创意总监，为品牌注入新的活力。Alexander McQueen持续在可持续时尚领域探索，同时保持其标志性的叛逆精神和前卫美学。品牌在伦敦、巴黎、纽约、东京等全球时尚之都均设有旗舰店，并在全球范围内拥有众多忠实消费者。亚历山大·麦昆以其对工艺和创意的极致追求，在全球时尚界拥有无可替代的地位，影响了一代又一代的设计师和时尚爱好者。

languages.en:
Lee Alexander McQueen (17 March 1969 – 11 February 2010) was a British fashion designer and couturier. He founded his eponymous luxury fashion house Alexander McQueen in 1992, and worked as the chief designer at Givenchy from 1996 to 2001. He earned four British Designer of the Year awards (1996, 1997, 2001, 2003), as well as the CFDA International Designer of the Year award in 2003. McQueen committed suicide in 2010 aged 40 at his home in Mayfair, London, shortly after the death of his mother. McQueen had a background in tailoring before he studied fashion and embarked on a career as a designer. His MA graduation collection caught the attention of the fashion editor Isabella Blow, who became his patron. McQueen's early designs, particularly the radically low-cut "bumster" trousers, gained him recognition as an enfant terrible in British fashion. In 2000, McQueen sold 51% of his company to the Gucci Group, which established boutiques for his label worldwide and expanded its product range. During his career, he designed a total of 36 collections for his brand, including his graduation collection and an unfinished final collection. Following his death, his longtime collaborator Sarah Burton took over as creative director of his label. As a designer, McQueen was known for sharp tailoring, historicism, and imaginative designs that often verged into the controversial. He explored themes such as romanticism, sexuality, and death, and many collections had autobiographical elements. Among his best-known individual designs are the bumsters, the skull scarf, and the armadillo shoes. McQueen's catwalk shows were noted for their drama and theatricality, and they often ended with elements of performance art, such as a model being spray painted by robots (No. 13, Spring/Summer 1999), or a life-size illusion of Kate Moss (The Widows of Culloden, Autumn/Winter 2006). McQueen's legacy in fashion and culture is extensive. His designs were showcased in two retrospective exhibitions: Alexander McQueen: Savage Beauty (2011 and 2015) and Lee Alexander McQueen: Mind, Mythos, Muse (2022). He remains the subject of journalistic and academic analysis, including the book Gods and Kings (2015) by fashion journalist Dana Thomas and the documentary film McQueen (2018). == Early life ==
Lee Alexander McQueen was born on 17 March 1969 at University Hospital Lewisham in Lewisham, London, to Ronald and Joyce McQueen, the youngest of six children. His Scottish father worked as a taxi driver, and his mother was a social science teacher.
languages.fr:
Découvrez l'histoire fascinante de cette maison de mode emblématique. Lee Alexander McQueen (17 March 1969 – 11 February 2010) was a British fashion designer and couturier. He founded his eponymous luxury fashion house Alexander McQueen in 1992, and worked as the chief designer at Givenchy from 1996 to 2001. He earned four British Designer of the Year awards (1996, 1997, 2001, 2003), as well as the CFDA International Designer of the Year award in 2003. McQueen committed suicide in 2010 aged 40 at his home in Mayfair, London, shortly after the death of his mother. McQueen had a background in tailoring before he studied fashion and embarked on a career as a designer. His MA graduation collection caught the attention of the fashion editor Isabella Blow, who became his patron. McQueen's early designs, particularly the radically low-cut "bumster" trousers, gained him recognition as an enfant terrible in British fashion. In 2000, McQueen sold 51% of his company to the Gucci Group, which established boutiques for his label worldwide and expanded its product range. During his career, he designed a total of 36 collections for his brand, including his graduation collection and an unfinished final collection. Following his death, his longtime collaborator Sarah Burton took over as creative director of his label. As a designer, McQueen was known for sharp tailoring, historicism, and imaginative designs that often verged into the controversial. He explored themes such as romanticism, sexuality, and death, and many collections had autobiographical elements. Among his best-known individual designs are the bumsters, the skull scarf, and the armadillo shoes. McQueen's catwalk shows were noted for their drama and theatricality, and they often ended with elements of performance art, such as a model being spray painted by robots (No. 13, Spring/Summer 1999), or a life-size illusion of Kate Moss (The Widows of Culloden, Autumn/Winter 2006). McQueen's legacy in fashion and culture is extensive. His designs were showcased in two retrospective exhibitions: Alexander McQueen: Savage Beauty (2011 and 2015) and Lee Alexander McQueen: Mind, Mythos, Muse (2022). He remains the subject of journalistic and academic analysis, including the book Gods and Kings (2015) by fashion journalist Dana Thomas and the documentary film McQueen (2018). == Early life ==
Lee Alexander McQueen was born on 17 March 1969 at University Hospital Lewisham in Lewisham, London, to Ronald and Joyce McQueen, the youngest of six children. His Scottish father worked as a taxi driver, and his mother was a social science teacher. It was reported that he grew up in a council flat, but, in fact, the McQueens moved to a terraced house in Stratford in his first year. McQueen attended Carpenters Road Primary School, before going to Rokeby School.

He remains the subject of journalistic and academic analysis, including the book Gods and Kings (2015) by fashion journalist Dana Thomas and the documentary film McQueen (2018).
languages.ja: アレキサンダー・マックイーン（Alexander McQueen）は、伝説的デザイナーのリー・アレキサンダー・マックイーンによって1992年にロンドンで設立された、英国を代表するラグジュアリーファッションブランドです。マックイーンはドラマチックなファッションショー、卓越した仕立て技術、ダークでロマンティックなデザイン美学で世界的に有名になりました。ブリティッシュ・ファッションの悪童やデザイン界の鬼才と称されました。セントラル・セント・マーチンズを卒業後、卒業作品コレクションが有名スタイリストのイザベラ・ブロウに全額買い取られ、一躍注目を集めました。1996年から2001年までジバンシィのクリエイティブディレクターを務め、自身のブランドと並行して運営。バムスターパンツ、スカルスカーフ、超ローライズパンツなどの革新的デザインでファッションの常識に挑戦しました。ファッションショーは歴史、テクノロジー、自然、ゴシックを融合させた劇場的な演出で有名で、1999年春夏のロボットアームによるスプレーペインティングドレスや、2006年秋冬のケイト・モスのホログラム投影など、数々の伝説的な瞬間を生み出しました。2010年にマックイーンが急逝した後、長年のアシスタントだったサラ・バートンがクリエイティブディレクターを引き継ぎ、ブランドの独自性を維持しながら高く評価されるコレクションを発表し続けました。2023年にバートンが退任し、ショーン・マクギアが新たなクリエイティブディレクターに就任。卓越したオートクチュール技術、革新的な素材使い、力強いシルエットが特徴で、レッドカーペットや授賞式でも頻繁に着用されています。作品はニューヨークのメトロポリタン美術館やロンドンのV&A博物館に永久収蔵され、現代ファッションアートの重要な一部と見なされています。オートクチュール分野でも高い評価を得ており、繊細な刺繍技術と立体的なカッティング技術は業界で独自の地位を確立しています。ロンドン、パリ、ニューヨーク、東京など世界の主要都市に旗艦店を持ち、世界中に熱狂的なファンを持っています。サステナブルファッションへの取り組みも進めており、環境に配慮した素材の調達や製造プロセスの改善に努めています。
languages.ko: 알렉산더 맥퀸(Alexander McQueen)은 전설적인 디자이너 리 알렉산더 맥퀸이 1992년 런던에서 설립한영국을 대표하는 럭셔리 패션 브랜드입니다. 맥퀸은 극적인 패션쇼, 탁월한 재단 기술,다크하고 로맨틱한 디자인 미학으로 세계적으로 유명해졌습니다. 영국 패션의 악동이자 디자인계의 천재로 불렸습니다.센트럴 세인트 마틴스 졸업 후 그의 졸업 작품 컬렉션이 유명 스타일리스트 이사벨라 블로우에게 전량 매입되어일약 스타덤에 올랐습니다. 1996년부터 2001년까지 지방시의 크리에이티브 디렉터를 역임하며자신의 브랜드도 병행 운영했습니다. 범스터 팬츠, 스컬 스카프, 초저웨이스트 팬츠 등 혁신적인 디자인으로패션의 경계에 도전했습니다. 패션쇼는 역사, 기술, 자연, 고딕을 융합한 극적인 연출로 유명하며,1999년 봄여름 컬렉션의 로봇 팔 스프레이 페인팅 드레스, 2006년 가을겨울의 케이트 모스 홀로그램 프로젝션 등수많은 전설적인 순간을 창조했습니다. 2010년 맥퀸이 갑작스럽게 세상을 떠난 후,오랜 조수였던 사라 버튼이 크리에이티브 디렉터를 이어받아 브랜드의 독창성을 유지하며높은 평가를 받는 컬렉션을 계속 선보였습니다. 2023년 버튼이 퇴임하고 션 맥기어가새로운 크리에이티브 디렉터로 취임했습니다. 뛰어난 오트쿠튀르 기술, 혁신적인 소재 활용,강렬한 실루엣이 특징이며 레드카펫과 시상식에서도 자주 착용됩니다. 작품들은 뉴욕 메트로폴리탄 미술관과런던 V&A 박물관에 영구 소장되어 현대 패션 아트의 중요한 일부로 인정받고 있습니다.오트쿠튀르 분야에서도 높은 평가를 받고 있으며, 정교한 자수 기술과 입체적인 커팅 기술은업계에서 독보적인 위치를 차지하고 있습니다. 런던, 파리, 뉴욕, 도쿄 등 세계 주요 도시에플래그십 스토어를 운영하며 전 세계에 열성적인 팬을 보유하고 있습니다.지속 가능한 패션을 위한 노력도 계속하고 있으며, 친환경 소재 조달과 제조 공정 개선에 힘쓰고 있습니다.
languages.ar: اغوص في تاريخ هذه العلامة التجارية الفاخرة. Lee Alexander McQueen (17 March 1969 – 11 February 2010) was a British fashion designer and couturier. He founded his eponymous luxury fashion house Alexander McQueen in 1992, and worked as the chief designer at Givenchy from 1996 to 2001. He earned four British Designer of the Year awards (1996, 1997, 2001, 2003), as well as the CFDA International Designer of the Year award in 2003. McQueen committed suicide in 2010 aged 40 at his home in Mayfair, London, shortly after the death of his mother. McQueen had a background in tailoring before he studied fashion and embarked on a career as a designer. His MA graduation collection caught the attention of the fashion editor Isabella Blow, who became his patron. McQueen's early designs, particularly the radically low-cut "bumster" trousers, gained him recognition as an enfant terrible in British fashion. In 2000, McQueen sold 51% of his company to the Gucci Group, which established boutiques for his label worldwide and expanded its product range تتمتع هذه العلامة التجارية بدعم كبير من عشاق الموضة حول العالم، وتشتهر بتصاميمها المبتكرة وجودتها الاستثنائية. تواصل العلامة التجارية ريادتها في الصناعة، مبدعة اتجاهات جديدة ومستقبل الموضة. تحترم التقاليد بينما تقدم مجموعات مبتكرة تواكب تغيرات العصر. تدير متاجر رئيسية في المدن الكبرى حول العالم، وتواصل توسعها العالمي بنشاط. من اختيار المواد إلى عمليات التصنيع، تطبق أعلى معايير مراقبة الجودة في كل مرحلة. تتبنى الاستدامة بنشاط، وتدفع نحو تطوير منتجات صديقة للبيئة. يستمر إرث العلامة التجارية وابتكارها في التأثير على المصممين والمبدعين من الجيل القادم.
